2605.06368.title: eXplaining to Learn (eX2L): Regularization Using Contrastive Visual Explanation Pairs for Distribution Shifts
2605.06368.paper_summary: Despite extensive research into mitigating distribution shifts, many existing algorithms yield inconsistent performance, often failing to outperform baseline Empirical Risk Minimization (ERM) across diverse scenarios. Furthermore, high algorithmic complexity frequently limits interpretability and offers only an indirect means of addressing spurious correlations. We propose eXplaining to Learn (eX2L): an interpretable, explanation-based framework that decorrelates confounding features from a classifier's latent representations during training. eX2L achieves this by penalizing the similarity between Grad-CAM activation maps generated by a primary label classifier and those from a concurrently trained confounder classifier. On the rigorous Spawrious Many-to-Many Hard Challenge benchmark, eX2L achieves an average accuracy (AA) of 82.24% +/- 3.87% and a worst-group accuracy (WGA) of 66.31% +/- 8.73%, outperforming the current state-of-the-art (SOTA) by 5.49% and 10.90%, respectively. Beyond its competitive performance, eX2L demonstrates that functional domain invariance can be achieved by explicitly decoupling label and nuisance attributes at the group level.
2605.06368.paper_summary_zh: 儘管對於減輕分佈偏移的研究相當廣泛，但許多現有的算法在不同場景中表現不一致，經常無法超越基線的經驗風險最小化（ERM）。此外，高算法複雜性常常限制了解釋性，並僅提供間接的方式來解決虛假相關性。我們提出了 eXplaining to Learn（eX2L）：一個可解釋的、基於解釋的框架，該框架在訓練過程中將混淆特徵與分類器的潛在表示解耦。eX2L 通過懲罰由主要標籤分類器生成的 Grad-CAM 激活圖與同時訓練的混淆分類器生成的激活圖之間的相似性來實現這一點。在嚴格的 Spawrious Many-to-Many Hard Challenge 基準上，eX2L 的平均準確率（AA）為 82.24% +/- 3.87%，最差組準確率（WGA）為 66.31% +/- 8.73%，分別超越當前的最先進技術（SOTA）5.49% 和 10.90%。除了其競爭力的表現外，eX2L 還表明，通過在組層面明確解耦標籤和干擾屬性，可以實現功能域不變性。
2605.06368.author: Paulo Mario P. Medina et.al.
2605.06368.authors: Paulo Mario P. Medina, Jose Marie Antonio Miñoza, Sebastian C. Ibañez
